perturbation.original_text: The term “Evaluation Material” does not include, however, information which (A) is or becomes generally available to the public through no fault of (including any breach of this Agreement by) the receiving party or any of its Representatives, (B) was within the receiving party’s possession prior to its being furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party, provided that the receiving party was not aware (after reasonable due inquiry) that the source of such information was bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing party, (C) is or becomes available to the receiving party or its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a source other 3101 Western Ave, Seattle, WA 98121 TEL (206) 315-7500 FAX (206) 315-7501 www.isilon.com Confidentiality Agreement August 29, 2010 than the disclosing party or its Representatives, provided the receiving party was not aware (after reasonable due inquiry) that such source was bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing party with respect to such information, (D) is independently developed by the receiving party without use of or reference to any Evaluation Material, or (E) is required to be disclosed to a third party under operation of applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “Evaluation Material” does not include, however, information which (A) is or becomes generally available to the public through no fault of the receiving party, (B) was within the receiving party’s possession prior to its being furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party, provided that the receiving party was not aware that the source of such information was bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing party, (C) is or becomes available to the receiving party on a non-confidential basis from a source other 3101 Western Ave, Seattle, WA 98121 TEL (206) 315-7500 FAX (206) 315-7501 www.isilon.com Confidentiality Agreement August 29, 2010 than the disclosing party, provided the receiving party was not aware that such source was bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing party with respect to such information, (D) is independently developed by the receiving party without use of or reference to any Evaluation Material, or (E) is required to be disclosed to a third party under operation of applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "(including any breach of this Agreement by) the receiving party or any of its Representatives" from (A) and "(after reasonable due inquiry)" from (B) and (C), there is a contradiction with section (3) Non-Disclosure and Use of Evaluation Material. Now, if the information becomes public due to a breach by a representative, it is no longer considered Evaluation Material, even though the representatives are supposed to keep it confidential. Also removing the need for "reasonable due inquiry" reduces the burden of the receiving party to ensure confidentiality of the information received.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (3) Non-Disclosure and Use of Evaluation Material . Each party agrees that it shall (and it shall cause its Representatives to) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Transaction and for no other purpose, and that the receiving party shall (and shall cause its Representatives to) keep the Evaluation Material confidential and not disclose any Evaluation Material to any third party; provided , however , that the receiving party may disclose any Evaluation Material (x) if the disclosing party gives its prior express written consent to this disclosure, or (y) to its Representatives who are actively or directly participating in its evaluation of a Transaction or who otherwise need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating a Transaction (and the receiving party shall take appropriate measures to ensure that its Representatives comply with the terms of this Agreement).
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase '(including any breach of this Agreement by) the receiving party or any of its Representatives' and '(after reasonable due inquiry)' from the definition of 'Evaluation Material'. This contradicts section (3) which requires the receiving party to ensure its Representatives keep the Evaluation Material confidential. Now, if a representative breaches the agreement and makes the information public, it's no longer considered Evaluation Material under the changed text, directly conflicting with the confidentiality obligations outlined in section (3). Also the removal of 'after reasonable due inquiry' makes the receiving party less responsible for ensuring confidentiality of the information received, contradicting section 3 as well.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for any Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered, and until such time, neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands and agrees that a preliminary agreement providing for any Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties once both parties express written intent, even if a final definitive agreement has not been executed and delivered, and until such time, neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to section (8) states that a preliminary agreement exists upon written intent, which contradicts the original statement that no contract exists until a final definitive agreement is executed. This introduces ambiguity and contradicts the later statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (8) Definitive Agreements . Each party understands and agrees that a preliminary agreement providing for any Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties once both parties express written intent, even if a final definitive agreement has not been executed and delivered, and until such time, neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party reserves the right, in its sole and absolute discretion, to provide or not provide Evaluation Material to the other party or its Representatives under this Agreement, to reject any and all proposals made by the other party or any of its Representatives with regard to a Transaction between the parties, and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no contract exists until a final definitive agreement is executed. The changed text introduces the concept of a 'preliminary agreement' that exists upon written intent, even without a final agreement. This directly contradicts the original condition for a contract's existence. The contradicted text includes the changed text, emphasizing that the introduction of a preliminary agreement upon written intent directly clashes with the original condition that no agreement exists before a final definitive agreement is executed, causing a strong in-text contradiction as defined in the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: At any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party and its Representatives shall promptly deliver to the disclosing party or destroy all Evaluation Material (and all copies, reproductions thereof) furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files), provided, however, that, the receiving party or its Representatives shall not, in connection with the foregoing obligations, be required to identify or delete Evaluation Material held electronically in archive or backup systems in accordance with general systems archiving and backup policies.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time upon the reasonable request of either disclosing party, each receiving party shall use its best effort to promptly deliver to the disclosing party or destroy all Evaluation Material (and all copies, reproductions thereof) furnished to the receiving party pursuant hereto (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files).
perturbation.explanation: By modifying section (6), the requirement of returning or destroying evaluation material upon 'any' request is limited to 'reasonable' requests, and removes the obligation of the representatives to comply with this condition. Additionally, the 'shall' is replaced by 'best effort', making the obligation less stringent, and thus contradictory to the original text where the return or destroy was clearly stated and binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (6) Termination of Discussions . All Evaluation Material shall remain the property of the disclosing party and the receiving party agrees not to assert any claim of title or ownership in the disclosing party’s Evaluation Material or any portion thereof. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Transaction, such party shall promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving a written notice of termination. At any time upon the reasonable request of either disclosing party, each receiving party shall use its best effort to promptly deliver to the disclosing party or destroy all Evaluation Material (and all copies, reproductions thereof) furnished to the receiving party pursuant hereto (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files). Upon the request of the disclosing party, a duly authorized representative of the receiving party shall certify to the disclosing party that the receiving party and its Representatives have complied with this paragraph (6). Notwithstanding the return or destruction of all Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section (6) by replacing 'any' request with 'reasonable' request and 'shall' with 'best effort', and also removes obligations of representatives. This directly contradicts the original intent of the Contradicted Text, which mandates that 'All Evaluation Material shall remain the property of the disclosing party' and creates an obligation to return/destroy material. The change makes this obligation less stringent ('best effort') and only applicable to 'reasonable' requests, therefore, there is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the other party or any of its Representatives and that the non-breaching party would be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction and/or specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands and agrees that money damages may be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the other party and that the non-breaching party would be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction and/or specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, in lieu of all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'money damages may not be a sufficient remedy' to 'money damages may be a sufficient remedy' and stating 'Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies' contradicts the original statement that the other party can seek other remedies. The modification limits recourse for a breach, creating internal contradiction regarding what legal actions can be pursued.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (13) Injunctive Relief . Each party understands and agrees that money damages may be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the other party and that the non-breaching party would be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction and/or specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, in lieu of all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'money damages may not be a sufficient remedy' to 'money damages may be a sufficient remedy' and further states that equitable remedies 'shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies'. This directly contradicts the initial understanding that other remedies might be available, thus limiting the recourse for a breach. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, solidifying that a contradiction now exists where previously there was not one.
